name: ComponentInput
summary: The ComponentInput class handles the input of component objects and positions. A component has one component input. The component input must include all the needed input objects and points in the correct order. For example, an input with a point and a part is different from an input of a part and a point. The maximum number of different input items in the collection is 10.

constructors.name: ComponentInput
constructors.summary: Creates a new component input object.

methods.name: AddInputObject
methods.summary: Adds a model object to the component input.

methods.name: AddInputObjects
methods.summary: Adds an array list of model objects to the component input.

methods.name: AddInputPolygon
methods.summary: Adds a polygon to the component input.

methods.name: AddOneInputPosition
methods.summary: Adds an input position to the component input.

methods.name: AddTwoInputPositions
methods.summary: Adds two input positions to the component input.

methods.name: CopyTo
methods.summary: Copies the elements of the ICollection to an Array, starting at a particular Array index.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: Returns an enumerator that iterates through a collection.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Gets the number of elements contained in the ICollection.

properties.name: IsSynchronized
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether access to the ICollection is synchronized (thread safe).

properties.name: SyncRoot
properties.summary: Gets an object that can be used to synchronize access to the ICollection.
